verdict_reason: The engineering layer preserves the technical Sanskrit term prajñā in the body of the analog ('That faculty is what the verse calls prajñā') with explicit retention. The mind-as-passive-being-led structure (anuvidhīyate as passive: the mind is led, not leading) is preserved through 'the mind, un-yoked, is drawn along behind any one of them' — directionally correct. The wind-boat metaphor is preserved structurally: a moving boat with intent (the boat is not weak — the boat is moving with intent), wind entering the sail (the wind has only to enter the sail), course-deflection (deflected from its course). No tradition is conflated. Shankara's specific reading of prajñā as ātma-anātma-viveka is not invoked, but neither is Ramanuja's or Prabhupada's — the engineering layer scopes prajñā as 'discriminative faculty' / 'discriminative judgment', the universal-consensus reading at that level of abstraction. The doctrinal axis (Advaita's ātma-anātma vs Vishishtadvaita's Self-as-locus-of-devotion vs Gaudiya's Lord-orientation) is not flattened into one tradition; it is correctly held above by the source pack and not engaged by the engineering layer. The falsifiability clause ('the verse names a structural mechanism — un-yoked mind drawn behind un-restrained sense — not an injunction toward total isolation') correctly guards against the asceticism misread that a careless engineering reading would invite. Pass.
